pattern: Walk the list collecting nodes into successively longer groups (1, 2, 3, ...), and splice a group's own segment backwards in place whenever the group's actual node count — which may be cut short by the list ending — turns out even.
hints: Track a `prev` pointer sitting just before the current group and grow the intended group size by one after every group, but stop collecting a group early if the list runs out of nodes.
Decide even-or-odd from how many nodes you actually collected for this group, not from the intended group size — a group cut short by the end of the list is judged by its real length.

When a group is even, reverse just that collected slice of pointers and reconnect `prev` to the new front of the slice and the old front of the slice to whatever node came after the group; odd groups are left untouched and `prev` simply moves to the group's last collected node.

edge_inputs.description: ten nodes spanning four groups, exercising both reversed and untouched groups together
the final group is shortened to exactly one node, staying odd and untouched
the final group is shortened but its actual length lands on two, so it still gets reversed
a four-node final group shortened down to a single leftover node, which stays odd and untouched
a single-node list is just one odd group of length one, so the output equals the input
the list ends exactly on a group boundary after three full groups
a longer list spanning five full groups plus a shortened, odd-length final group
every node shares the same value, so only position (not value) distinguishes the reversal

stress.description: random-length lists with random values, sized up to a couple thousand nodes to exercise many group boundaries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 2000)))
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def reverseEvenLengthGroups(self, head):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        result = []
        i = 0
        group_len = 1
        while i < len(vals):
            chunk = vals[i:i + group_len]
            if len(chunk) % 2 == 0:
                chunk = chunk[::-1]
            result.extend(chunk)
            i += group_len
            group_len += 1
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        tail = dummy
        for v in result:
            tail.next = ListNode(v)
            tail = tail.next
        return dummy.next

complexity.space: O(sqrt(n)) auxiliary for the largest single group buffer
